sections.title: Introduction to Subject Pronouns
sections.content: Subject pronouns replace the subject of a sentence (the person or thing performing the action). In French, subject pronouns must be used with every conjugated verb.
examples: Je parle (I speak)
Tu parles (You speak - informal singular)
Il/Elle/On parle (He/She/One speaks)
Nous parlons (We speak)
Vous parlez (You speak - formal or plural)
Ils/Elles parlent (They speak)

sections.title: Singular Subject Pronouns
sections.content: Je (I), tu (you informal), il (he), elle (she), on (one/we/they). Note that on can refer to people in general, or be equivalent to nous in informal speech.
examples: Je suis étudiant (I am a student)
Tu es sympa (You are nice)
Il a un chat (He has a cat)
Elle va au cinéma (She goes to the cinema)
On mange à 19h (We/They eat at 7pm)

sections.title: Plural Subject Pronouns
sections.content: Nous (we), vous (you formal or plural), ils (they masculine or mixed), elles (they feminine).
examples: Nous allons au parc (We go to the park)
Vous avez raison (You are right)
Ils jouent au football (They play football)
Elles sont parties (They left - feminine)

sections.title: Formal vs Informal
sections.content: Tu is used for informal singular 'you' (friends, family, children). Vous is used for formal singular 'you' (strangers, elders, superiors) and for all plural 'you' (multiple people).
examples: Tu vas bien? (Are you okay? - to a friend)
Vous allez bien, monsieur? (Are you okay, sir? - formal)
Vous allez où? (Where are you going? - to multiple people)

sections.title: Pronouns with Verb Conjugation
sections.content: Each subject pronoun requires a different verb conjugation. This is why it's essential to memorize verb conjugations with their pronouns.
examples: Parler: je parle, tu parles, il parle, nous parlons, vous parlez, ils parlent
Être: je suis, tu es, il est, nous sommes, vous êtes, ils sont
Avoir: j'ai, tu as, il a, nous avons, vous avez, ils ont

sections.title: Omitting Subject Pronouns
sections.content: In French, subject pronouns cannot be omitted (unlike in some other languages). Every verb must have an explicit subject pronoun.
examples: Correct: Je parle (I speak)
Incorrect: *Parle (Speak) - without subject
In imperative: Parle! (Speak!) - but this is a different mood

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Write a short dialogue between three people using different subject pronouns. Make sure each line has the correct pronoun and verb conjugation.
